assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response mentions checking deprecated APIs but focuses on 1.32 removals, not behavior changes in 1.30 which is irrelevant to a 1.31→1.32 upgrade

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: No mention of admission webhooks or checking for webhook-related issues that could cause latency or 503 errors

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: While it checks resource utilization, it doesn't specifically advise adjusting resource requests/limits for system components that may have changed requirements in 1.32

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check kube-system pods and look for restarts, specifically 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver' and monitoring commands

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Explicitly includes 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_duration' command to check API server performance

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Mentions checking CNI plugins and service mesh components like Istio, Linkerd, and includes restart commands for ingress controllers

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: No specific mention of checking pod events with kubectl describe pods or kubectl get events to look for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Includes HPA-related troubleshooting with commands to temporarily disable HPA and mentions checking if HPA is causing issues
